agent: PRN Negeri Sembilan 2026 Sentiment Analysis Agent (cron)
status: completed (revision 5 — Cycle 7 / 02:35 MYT 19 Jul deep-overnight delta merge of the 170837 cycle PIR-06 ESCALATION + 182751 stability confirmation)
method: agent-manual source-attributed review (CVS-compliant) + SPR official candidate-list cross-reference + director-approved PIR priority weighting

election_milestone: CAMPAIGN PERIOD DAY 1 (Nomination Day 18 Jul 2026) — DEEP-OVERNIGHT CONFIRMATION CYCLE (02:35 MYT 19 Jul). Polling day 1 Aug 2026; early voting 28 Jul 2026; BN manifesto launch 24 Jul (DUN Linggi). Electorate 889,490.

priority_directive.statement: Weight sentiment analysis toward approved PIRs: PIR-06 (PN-Bersatu removal/expulsion/withdrawal narratives), PIR-09 (candidate credibility — disciplinary actions, defections, eligibility), PIR-07 (battleground seats — campaign momentum, candidate reception).
priority_directive.critical_flag_directive: Flag any CRITICAL sentiment shift related to PIR-06 (PN-removal-of-Bersatu).
priority_directive.shift_threshold: Flag any entity with sharp sentiment shift (>30% change from previous cycle).

prior_revision.coverage: 55 source files / 232 entities; cumulative 40 entities scored (15 pos / 14 neu / 11 neg)
prior_revision.key_finding: PIR-06 ESCALATED from precursor (rev3) to SENIOR-FIGURE PUBLIC ADVOCACY (Kiandee, suspended Bersatu VP). One-sided motion: Kiandee calls ON PN-MT to remove Bersatu. Trigger #2 adjacent.

The 170837 cycle is a SOURCE-GAP FILL + PIR-06 ESCALATION CORROBORATION cycle that captured the Bersatu PRESIDENT (Muhyiddin) publicly hinting at exit. The 182751 cycle is a STABILITY/CONFIRMATION pass (0 new articles, 10/10 sources, 0 errors). The entities_165723.json (16:57 MYT) provides the PIR-tagged entity structure (8 Tier-4 seats, PIR-09 candidates, PIR-07 watch seats) cross-referenced here.

New priority files: 6 (priority_pir06_goodbye-pn-muhyiddin / priority_pir06_bersatu-contest-own-flag / priority_pir06_bersatu-exit-pn-imminent-snapshot / priority_pir06_bersatu-tidak-lagi-mahu-bersama-pn-ronald / priority_pir09_mca-youth-stay-out-ns-polls / priority_pir09_pn-camp-albert-barking-dogs).

STABILITY/CONFIRMATION pass — deep-overnight MYT, 10/10 sources, 0 errors, 0 new articles. PIR-06 escalated-watch assessment UNCHANGED from 170837. Value = coverage integrity + assessment stability confirmation.

source_basis_delta.cvs_standard: Sentiment based on verified source content only. No pattern-inferred sentiment. All entities source-attributed (verbatim surface match). SPR candidate list = ground truth. PIR priority pointer files + collection_metadata trajectory assessment used only to LOCATE items; media source files cited as attribution per CVS standard. The 182751 stability-confirmation cycle (0 new articles) reinforces that no regression or CRITICAL threshold crossing occurred in the deep-overnight window.

negative.entity: [Rev5Δ][PIR-06 CRITICAL] Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin (Bersatu PRESIDENT) — publicly hints at Bersatu EXIT from PN ('Goodbye PN?')
negative.category: politician (Bersatu president) — PIR-06 principal-level exit signal
negative.trend: NEW — CRITICAL ESCALATION. Revision-4 had the PIR-06 motion as ONE-SIDED: Kiandee (a SUSPENDED VP) calling ON the PN-MT to remove Bersatu. Rev5: the Bersatu PRESIDENT (Muhyiddin) now publicly hints at exit — 'wishing PN all the best', rapping PN chairperson Samsuri for 'unilaterally deciding to enter an electoral pact with BN and sidelining Bersatu from seat negotiations.' This is the PRINCIPAL signaling departure, not a suspended figure calling for removal. The motion is now TWO-SIDED: PN-MT-may-remove-Bersatu (Kiandee) AND Bersatu-may-exit-PN (Muhyiddin).
negative.rationale: Malaysiakini (mkini news/779941, captured 170837 cycle): 'After months of escalating tensions with ally PAS, Bersatu president Muhyiddin Yassin today hinted at the party's possible exit from Perikatan Nasional. In a statement wishing PN all the best, Muhyiddin hit out at coalition chairperson Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar for unilaterally deciding to enter an electoral pact with BN and sidelining Bersatu from seat negotiations.' PLUS The Star (170837): Muhyiddin reframes Bersatu as 'sole true opposition' post BN-PN pact (consistent with exit positioning) AND signals a NEW coalition/bloc AFTER the Negri polls (concrete post-exit plan). This is the HIGHEST-WEIGHT PIR-06 development of the cycle — the principal's exit-hint moves the coalition-restructuring motion from one-sided advocacy to two-sided departure signaling. Maximally favourable to PH: a Bersatu exit/realignment further fragments the opposition. STATUS: no formal exit notice filed; this is a public hint/statement, not a formal withdrawal. Trigger #2 (coalition restructuring) is CLOSER than rev4 — the restructuring can now occur via EITHER path.
negative.source_reliability: HIGH — Malaysiakini (richest PRN source, 33 articles indexed), The Star corroborating, collection_metadata trajectory assessment. Full bodies paywalled (Malaysiakini lead para ~71 words) but headline + lead + multi-source corroboration confirm the signal.
negative.critical_flag: CRITICAL — PIR-06 (PN-removal-of-Bersatu) sentiment shift. Per director directive: FLAGGED. The qualitative escalation (one-sided suspended-VP advocacy → two-sided president-level exit signaling) is the cycle's most material sentiment shift. The numeric score (0.80) vs the rev4 Kiandee entity (0.76) is a +5.3% delta (below the 30% threshold), but the QUALITATIVE shift (principal-level, two-sided) is FLAGGED as CRITICAL. See sentiment_shift_flags.

negative.entity: [Rev5Δ][PIR-06] Tan Sri Annuar Musa (Bersatu sec-gen) — states Bersatu wants to part ways with PN (own-logo signal)
negative.category: politician (Bersatu secretary-general) — PIR-06 corroborating exit signal
negative.trend: NEW — Bersatu sec-gen Annuar Musa publicly states Bersatu's use of its own logo in the NS polls shows it wants to part ways with PN. A second Bersatu-leadership figure (after Muhyiddin) confirming the departure trajectory. Annuar Musa appears in the entities_165723.json PIR-07 entities list (line 2834) — bridges PIR-06 and PIR-07.
negative.rationale: The Star (170837): 'Negri polls: Bersatu's use of own logo shows it wants to part ways with PN, says Annuar Musa.' Combined with Muhyiddin's exit-hint, this is a COORDINATED Bersatu-leadership signal: president + sec-gen both publicly framing the own-logo decision as a parting-of-ways. The own-logo contest (24 Bersatu candidates under the Bersatu flag, not the PN logo) is the operational signature of the rupture (SPR-confirmed). Negative for PN cohesion; corroborates the PIR-06 two-sided motion.
negative.source_reliability: HIGH — The Star gnews feed; Annuar Musa on record as Bersatu sec-gen.

negative.entity: [Rev5Δ][PIR-06] PAS openly sidelining Bersatu — 'PN rift widens' + Hadi blame-shifts ('blames Bersatu instead')
negative.category: party/issue (PIR-06 — PAS side of the Bersatu-PN fracture)
negative.trend: NEW — The PAS side of the fracture now openly active. Malaysiakini (170837): 'PN rift widens as PAS sends clearest signal yet of sidelining Bersatu.' PLUS FMT (170837, 18:48 MYT): 'Hadi denies PAS made PN toxic, blames Bersatu instead' — Hadi (PIR-06 monitor target) deflects the 'toxic' label onto Bersatu, blame-shifting consistent with the PAS-sidelining-Bersatu framing. This completes the three-way fracture picture: Bersatu (Muhyiddin) signaling exit; PN-MT (Kiandee call) signaling removal; PAS openly sidelining + blame-shifting.
negative.rationale: The fracture is now manifest on ALL THREE sides simultaneously: (1) Bersatu-leadership (Muhyiddin + Annuar Musa) signaling exit; (2) PN-MT-removal call (Kiandee); (3) PAS openly sidelining + Hadi blame-shifting. This is the strongest structural confirmation that the Bersatu-PN coalition restructuring is in motion, even though no formal PN-MT removal notice has issued. Hadi is a named PIR-06 monitor target (entities_165723.json: watch_figures_next_cycle). Negative for opposition cohesion; maximally favourable to PH.
negative.source_reliability: HIGH — Malaysiakini (rift-widening) + FMT (Hadi denial) via gnews universal feed; multi-source.

negative.entity: [Rev5Δ][PIR-09] Saw Yee Fung (MCA Youth sec-gen) — told to 'stay out of NS polls' over BN-PN criticism (internal BN-component disciplinary silencing)
negative.trend: NEW — THIRD PN/BN-component disciplinary action of the cycle (after Kiandee/Bersatu signal rev4 and Gerakan pecat of Tang Jay Son rev4). MCA allegedly instructed its Youth wing sec-gen Saw Yee Fung to skip the NS campaign after she openly criticised BN's decision to cooperate with PN. MCA sec-gen Chong Sin Woon's WhatsApp: 'you don't have to come.' She revealed this in a lengthy Facebook post with a screenshot.
negative.rationale: Malaysiakini (mkini news/780017, 170837): internal BN-component disciplinary silencing of a dissenting Youth leader over the BN-PN pact. NOT a candidate expulsion (cf. Gerakan pecat of Tang Jay Son), but a candidate-campaign EXCLUSION. Adds to the 'PN/BN-component disciplinary actions' pattern and reveals intra-unity-government tension over the BN-PN electoral understanding. Negative for BN-component cohesion; a PH-attack vector (BN silencing internal dissent to accommodate PN). Watch: whether Saw defies the exclusion or further BN-component cracks emerge.
negative.source_reliability: HIGH — Malaysiakini; Saw Yee Fung's own Facebook post + WhatsApp screenshot (self-disclosed).

negative.entity: [Rev5Δ][PIR-09] Albert Tei (PN camp, Jelebu) — 'barking dogs' rhetoric toward PH supporters at Dewan Besar Kuala Klawang
negative.category: politician/figure (PN camp) — candidate credibility / messaging tone
negative.trend: NEW — Day-1 PN-camp abrasive rhetoric. Businessperson Albert Tei, dressed in a PN shirt, targeted PH supporters gathering outside the Dewan Besar Kuala Klawang nomination centre (Jelebu) and labelled them 'barking dogs.' A 25-second video clip shows Tei pointing toward the PH supporters: 'Look here, there are many dogs... Do you know dogs? They are just barking.'
negative.rationale: Malaysiakini (mkini news/780042, 170837): an abrasive character/credibility signal on a PN-side figure at the Jelebu nomination centre (the same centre where BN's Jalaluddin led the berarak procession — rev4 positive). Negative for PN-camp messaging tone (PIR-09) — contrasts sharply with the civil 'friendship match' framing of PH's Azizul (rev4 positive). A PH-attack vector (PN toxicity/abrasiveness). Watch: whether this goes viral or draws PN-leadership distancing.

neutral.entity: [Rev5Δ][PIR-06] Mainstream 'Bersatu exit from PN imminent?' framing (Malaysiakini SNAPSHOT)
neutral.trend: NEW — Narrative-framing datum. A mainstream outlet (Malaysiakini SNAPSHOT, mkini news/780047) now frames the Bersatu exit as 'imminent?' — the media narrative has shifted from 'has grounds to remove' (rev3/4, removal-frame) to 'exit imminent?' (rev5, departure-frame). This is a media-narrative signal, not a statement by a political actor; logged as a neutral watch-item with high PIR-06 weight.
neutral.rationale: Malaysiakini SNAPSHOT (170837): 'Bersatu exit from PN imminent?' The question-mark framing indicates the mainstream press now treats exit as a live near-term possibility, not a hypothetical. This media-framing shift corroborates the two-sided motion (Muhyiddin exit-hint + Kiandee removal-call). Neutral as a media signal; its IMPLICATION is negative for PN cohesion. Watch: whether 'imminent' becomes 'announced' in the daytime MYT cycles (campaign Day-2, 19 Jul).
neutral.source_reliability: MEDIUM-HIGH — Malaysiakini SNAPSHOT (KINI snapshot format, updated 11am/2pm/6pm); headline-level.

neutral.entity: [Rev5Δ][PIR-07] BN-PN electoral understanding — confirmed operational and PAS-led (Hadi + Asyraf Wajdi + Zahid cluster)
neutral.trend: NEW — The BN-PN electoral understanding is confirmed operational with a cluster of statements: Hadi ('PN to assist BN during NS campaign'; 'friendship, brotherhood'), Asyraf Wajdi ('BN-PN understanding aimed at ensuring NS political stability'), Zahid ('new political alignments needed for stability'; 'jajaran politik baharu jika BN, PN menang'). The understanding is PAS-led on the PN side.
neutral.rationale: NST + Kosmo + Utusan (170837 cluster): the BN-PN electoral understanding is operational — PN/PAS will assist BN in the NS campaign, and both sides frame it as stability/friendship. This is a structural datum: the unity-government side (PH-BN) now faces a PN-PAS component that is cooperating with BN in NS (the same arrangement Muhyiddin says sidelines Bersatu). Neutral for the seat contests (BN-PN cooperation helps BN in Malay seats but raises PH-component tension); the PAS-led framing is a PIR-07 watch item. This is the backdrop against which the PIR-06 Bersatu-exit motion plays out.
neutral.source_reliability: HIGH — multi-source (NST/Kosmo/Utusan); multiple PN/BN figures on record.

neutral.entity: [Rev5Δ][PIR-07] DAP revives anti-BN campaign — PH-component tension with the BN-PN pact (1MDB, ruler crisis)
neutral.trend: NEW — DAP is reviving its anti-BN campaign ahead of the NS polls (raising the 1MDB scandal and the ruler crisis), creating intra-unity-government tension with the BN-PN electoral pact. This is the PH-component side of the BN-PN cooperation tension (cf. MCA silencing Saw Yee Fung on the BN side).
neutral.rationale: Malaysiakini (170837): DAP revives anti-BN campaign. While PH and BN are formally in the unity government and have an electoral understanding in NS, DAP is raising historical anti-BN attack lines (1MDB, ruler crisis). This is a two-sided tension: DAP (PH-component) attacks BN; MCA (BN-component) silences its own Youth leader for attacking PN. The BN-PN understanding holds at the leadership level but generates friction at the component level. Neutral-to-negative: watch whether this tension escalates into open coalition friction or remains managed. PIR-07 (battleground narrative) + PIR-09 (component discipline) overlap.

neutral.entity: [Rev5Δ][PIR-07] Ahmad Samsuri (PN chairperson) — 'PN may continue BN cooperation if NS results positive' (post-election coalition intent)
neutral.category: politician (PN chairperson) — PIR-07 post-election coalition intent
neutral.trend: NEW (extends rev4 Samsuri reference). Samsuri — the same figure Muhyiddin rapped for 'unilaterally deciding to enter an electoral pact with BN and sidelining Bersatu' — signals PN may CONTINUE the BN cooperation if the NS results prove positive. This is the PN/PAS side's post-election coalition intent, contingent on the NS result.
neutral.rationale: NST (pubdate 17 Jul 14:55 MYT, re-surfaced 170837 gnews): Samsuri's post-election intent statement. This is the OTHER side of the PIR-06 motion: while Bersatu (Muhyiddin) signals exit, PAS-PN (Samsuri) signals continuation with BN — contingent on a positive NS result. The BN-PN pact is the arrangement that excludes Bersatu. Neutral: a post-election contingent statement, not a Day-1 campaign event. PIR-07 (post-election coalition shape) + PIR-06 (Bersatu excluded from the continuation).
neutral.source_reliability: HIGH — NST gnews; Samsuri on record as PN chairperson.

sentiment_distribution.note: Revision-5 delta merge. The rev5 delta (0 pos / 4 neu / 5 neg) is WEIGHTED ENTIRELY toward the PIR-priority negative axis: ALL 5 new negatives are PIR-06 (3) or PIR-09 (2) priority items. The dominant development of the 170837 cycle is the ESCALATION of the PIR-06 PN-removal-of-Bersatu thread from ONE-SIDED senior-figure advocacy (rev4: Kiandee, suspended VP) to TWO-SIDED principal-level departure signaling (rev5: Muhyiddin, Bersatu PRESIDENT, publicly hints at exit + signals new post-election bloc + Annuar Musa corroborates + PAS openly sidelines + mainstream 'imminent?' framing). No new positive entities in this delta — the deep-overnight window produced no new PH-favorable campaign content (the PH positives are carryover from rev4: Aminuddin, Azizul, Jalaluddin, SPR-confirmed configuration). The 182751 cycle (02:27 MYT 19 Jul) is a STABILITY/CONFIRMATION pass: 0 new articles, 10/10 sources, 0 errors — PIR-06 escalated-watch assessment UNCHANGED from 170837. No nomination-period trigger fired; trigger #2 (coalition restructuring) is CLOSER than rev4 (two-sided motion).

pir_weighted_entity_scores.description: Entities tagged with director-approved PIR priority tags (PIR-06/09/07) receive a priority_weight multiplier (1.3–2.0) applied to the base score. weighted_score = score × priority_weight. Negative-sentiment entities with high weighted scores are the highest-priority watch items.

sentiment_shift_flags.numeric_shifts_note: No entity exceeds the 30% NUMERIC sentiment-shift threshold in the rev4→rev5 delta. The largest tracked numeric deltas are same-direction escalations well below threshold: ESC-014 (PN-removal-of-Bersatu thread) 0.76→0.82 (+7.9%); ESC-011 (Bersatu-PN fracture) 0.75→0.80 (+6.7%); Bersatu (party) 0.70→0.74 (+5.7%). No entity FLIPS direction (positive↔negative). The cycle's material movement is a QUALITATIVE escalation of the PIR-06 thread (one-sided suspended-VP advocacy → two-sided principal-level exit signaling) and the INTRODUCTION of new PIR-06/P-09 negative entities (Muhyiddin, Annuar Musa, PAS/Hadi, Saw Yee Fung, Albert Tei) — these have no prior score so a numeric delta is undefined (NEW).

critical_qualitative_shift.prior_state_rev4: ONE-SIDED senior-figure public advocacy. Kiandee (suspended/former Bersatu VP, MP Beluran) publicly advocates the PN Supreme Council remove Bersatu ('asas kukuh'). A suspended figure calling ON the PN-MT to act — the termination threat was senior-figure-led but one-directional (PN → Bersatu). Trigger #2 adjacent.
critical_qualitative_shift.new_state_rev5: TWO-SIDED principal-level departure signaling. The Bersatu PRESIDENT (Muhyiddin) now publicly hints at exit — wishing PN 'all the best', rapping PN chairperson Samsuri for unilaterally entering the BN pact and sidelining Bersatu, and signaling a NEW coalition/bloc after the NS polls. Bersatu sec-gen Annuar Musa corroborates (own-logo = part-ways). PAS openly sidelines Bersatu ('PN rift widens'); Hadi blame-shifts ('blames Bersatu instead'). Mainstream framing shifts to 'Bersatu exit from PN imminent?' The motion is now three-sided: PN-MT-may-remove-Bersatu (Kiandee) AND Bersatu-may-exit-PN (Muhyiddin/Annuar Musa) AND PAS-sidelining-Bersatu (Hadi). NO formal PN-MT removal notice and NO formal Bersatu withdrawal notice have issued — status remains precursor/lead/hint, not formal-notice.
critical_qualitative_shift.classification: CRITICAL PIR-06 SENTIMENT SHIFT (qualitative escalation from one-sided advocacy to two-sided/three-sided principal-level coalition-restructuring motion)
critical_qualitative_shift.director_directive_met: YES — 'Flag any CRITICAL sentiment shift related to PIR-06 (PN-removal-of-Bersatu)' — FLAGGED. This is the cycle's highest-priority alert and the second consecutive revision to escalate (rev3 precursor → rev4 senior-figure advocacy → rev5 two-sided principal-level exit signaling).
critical_qualitative_shift.trigger_proximity: CLOSER than rev4. Escalation trigger #2 (unannounced alliance / coalition restructuring / realignment) is now reachable via EITHER path: (a) PN-MT formal removal notice, or (b) Bersatu formal exit/realignment. Per collection_metadata trajectory assessment: the most likely near-term outcome is Bersatu voluntarily realigning/exiting AFTER the Aug 1 poll (post-election) rather than a formal PN-MT expulsion beforehand — but a reactive PN-MT removal notice remains possible if Bersatu candidates actively damage PN partners in the 8 Tier-4 seats. No trigger has fired.

PIR-06.directive: Sentiment around PN-Bersatu relationship (removal, expulsion, withdrawal narratives)
PIR-06.assessment: ESCALATING — TWO-SIDED principal-level motion. The PIR-06 thread has progressed across the day: 15:09 'could face termination' (Star ticker) → 16:33 'info chief floats termination' (summary) → 19:19 'has grounds to remove' (NST, Kiandee, headline) → 22:20 SENIOR-FIGURE PUBLIC ADVOCACY (Kiandee full Utusan body, 'asas kukuh') [rev4] → 01:08 MYT 19 Jul PRINCIPAL-LEVEL EXIT SIGNALING (Muhyiddin, Bersatu president, publicly hints at exit + signals new post-election bloc; Annuar Musa corroborates; PAS openly sidelines Bersatu; Hadi blame-shifts; mainstream 'imminent?' framing) [rev5]. The motion is now THREE-SIDED: PN-MT-may-remove-Bersatu (Kiandee) AND Bersatu-may-exit-PN (Muhyiddin/Annuar Musa) AND PAS-sidelining-Bersatu (Hadi). NO formal PN-MT removal notice and NO formal Bersatu withdrawal notice have issued — status remains precursor/lead/hint. Bersatu contests 24 seats under its own flag (SPR-confirmed) — the operational signature of the rupture. 8 Tier-4 seats mapped: 4 vs PAS (Serting, Ampangan, Paroi, Bagan Pinang) + 4 vs Wawasan (Klawang, Sikamat, Mambau, Gemas). Trajectory per collection_metadata: most likely near-term outcome is Bersatu voluntarily realigning/exiting AFTER the Aug 1 poll rather than a formal PN-MT expulsion beforehand — but a reactive PN-MT removal notice remains possible. Maximally favourable to PH.

PIR-06.escalation_flag: ESC-014 (CRITICAL, escalated rev3→rev4→rev5; now two-sided/three-sided principal-level)
PIR-06.watch: Track PN/Bersatu/PAS/Muhyiddin/Annuar Musa official channels + Kiandee social media (Facebook — CONFIRMED active) for: (1) ANY formal PN Supreme Council (Majlis Tertinggi) removal notice — keyword watch on 'mengeluarkan Bersatu daripada PN' issued BY the MT (not Kiandee's call ON the MT); (2) Muhyiddin follow-through on 'new bloc/coalition after Negri polls' — any concrete announcement of Bersatu's new alliance partners; (3) ANY Bersatu candidate tarik diri in the 8 Tier-4 seats (N.04/05/13/14/23/25/31/34) — would signal internal Bersatu collapse. Next meaningful update expected at daytime MYT cycles (campaign Day-2, 19 Jul).

PIR-09.directive: Sentiment around candidate credibility (disciplinary actions, defections, eligibility)
PIR-09.assessment: THREE candidate-credibility events now confirmed across the cycle: (1) Gerakan expels Tang Jay Son (carryover rev4, reconfirmed 170837/182751) for contesting under Bersatu ticket in N.22 Rahang; (2) Mohamad Rafie (2018 PH winner) cross-bloc defector to PN-PAS in N.14 Ampangan (carryover rev4, reconfirmed 170837); (3) NEW rev5: MCA Youth sec-gen Saw Yee Fung told to 'stay out of NS polls' by MCA sec-gen Chong Sin Woon over her public criticism of BN-PN cooperation — internal BN-component disciplinary silencing (candidate-campaign exclusion, not expulsion). PLUS NEW rev5: Albert Tei (PN camp, Jelebu) 'barking dogs' abrasive rhetoric toward PH supporters at the Dewan Besar Kuala Klawang nomination centre — a Day-1 character/credibility signal. PLUS carryover: Bujang Abu (Ind, N.13) eligibility failure (withdrew — incomplete SPR expenses report; marginal, NOT a trigger). The disciplinary-action pattern (Gerakan pecat + MCA silencing + Kiandee/Bersatu signal) supports the broader PIR-06 fracture: PN/BN components are moving against figures associated with the Bersatu breakaway or dissenting over the BN-PN pact.

PIR-07.assessment: Day-1 campaign activity confirmed across 8 watch seats (carryover rev4: Chennah, Rantau, Linggi, Ampangan, Sikamat, Klawang, Sri Tanjung, Nilai). NEW rev5 context: (1) BN-PN electoral understanding confirmed operational and PAS-led (Hadi/Asyraf Wajdi/Zahid cluster) — the structural backdrop against which the PIR-06 Bersatu-exit motion plays out; (2) DAP revives anti-BN campaign (1MDB, ruler crisis) — intra-unity-government PH-component tension with the BN-PN pact; (3) Samsuri signals PN may continue BN cooperation if NS results positive — post-election coalition intent contingent on the NS result. No new Day-1 campaign events or poll/forecast publications in the deep-overnight window (182751 cycle: 0 new articles). 25 of 36 seats multi-cornered (69%); 2 five-cornered (N.10 Nilai, N.33 Sri Tanjung) — trigger #7 WATCH (independents splitting decisive segments). BN momentum (carryover): Jalaluddin Jelebu procession + 'way forward' manifesto (launch 24 Jul DUN Linggi). PH momentum (carryover): Aminuddin defensive-governance + Azizul civil 'friendship match' + Fuziah measured strategy + Nazri 50/50 candour.

Bersatu PRESIDENT Muhyiddin publicly hints at exit (wishes PN 'all the best', raps Samsuri, signals new post-election bloc); Bersatu sec-gen Annuar Musa corroborates (own-logo = part-ways); PAS openly sidelines Bersatu ('PN rift widens'); Hadi blame-shifts; mainstream frames exit as 'imminent?' The motion now operates on all three sides: PN-MT-may-remove (Kiandee) + Bersatu-may-exit (Muhyiddin/Annuar Musa) + PAS-sidelining (Hadi).

trends.overall_rev5: PH-FAVORABLE TILT STRENGTHENS FURTHER; PIR-06 ESCALATES TO TWO-SIDED PRINCIPAL-LEVEL MOTION. The dominant development of the 170837 cycle is the ESCALATION of the PIR-06 PN-removal-of-Bersatu thread from ONE-SIDED senior-figure advocacy (rev4: Kiandee, suspended VP, calls ON PN-MT to remove Bersatu) to TWO-SIDED/THREE-SIDED principal-level coalition-restructuring motion (rev5: Bersatu PRESIDENT Muhyiddin publicly hints at exit + signals new post-election bloc + Annuar Musa corroborates + PAS openly sidelines Bersatu + Hadi blame-shifts + mainstream 'imminent?' framing). The PH-favorable structural tilt deepens further: the opposition is now fractured on ALL THREE sides simultaneously, and the Bersatu president's exit-hint is the strongest signal yet that the coalition restructuring is in motion. No formal notice of either type (PN-MT removal OR Bersatu withdrawal) has issued. PIR-09 adds a third disciplinary action (MCA silencing of Saw Yee Fung) and a Day-1 abrasive-rhetoric datum (Albert Tei 'barking dogs'). PIR-07 confirms the BN-PN electoral understanding is operational (PAS-led) and surfaces DAP anti-BN intra-unity tension. The 182751 cycle (02:27 MYT 19 Jul) is a STABILITY/CONFIRMATION pass: 0 new articles, 10/10 sources, 0 errors — the escalated-watch assessment is unchanged. No entity exceeds the 30% numeric shift threshold; the material movement is qualitative (PIR-06 two-sided escalation) and new PIR-09 entities.

verdict: CAMPAIGN DAY 1 DEEP-OVERNIGHT CONFIRMATION (02:35 MYT 19 Jul) ESCALATES the PIR-06 PN-removal-of-Bersatu thread to a TWO-SIDED/THREE-SIDED PRINCIPAL-LEVEL COALITION-RESTRUCTURING MOTION — the second consecutive revision to escalate. The Bersatu PRESIDENT Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin publicly hints at exit from PN (Malaysiakini news/779941, 170837 cycle): wishing PN 'all the best', rapping PN chairperson Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar for 'unilaterally deciding to enter an electoral pact with BN and sidelining Bersatu from seat negotiations', and signaling a NEW coalition/bloc AFTER the Negri Sembilan polls. Bersatu sec-gen Tan Sri Annuar Musa corroborates (The Star: 'Bersatu's use of own logo shows it wants to part ways with PN'). PAS openly sidelines Bersatu (Malaysiakini: 'PN rift widens as PAS sends clearest signal yet of sidelining Bersatu'); Hadi Awang blame-shifts (FMT: 'Hadi denies PAS made PN toxic, blames Bersatu instead'). Mainstream framing shifts to 'Bersatu exit from PN imminent?' (Malaysiakini SNAPSHOT). The motion is now THREE-SIDED: PN-MT-may-remove-Bersatu (Kiandee, carryover) + Bersatu-may-exit-PN (Muhyiddin/Annuar Musa) + PAS-sidelining-Bersatu (Hadi). NO formal PN Supreme Council removal notice and NO formal Bersatu withdrawal notice have issued — status remains precursor/lead/hint, not formal-notice. Per collection_metadata trajectory assessment, the most likely near-term outcome is Bersatu voluntarily realigning/exiting AFTER the Aug 1 poll rather than a formal PN-MT expulsion beforehand — but a reactive PN-MT removal notice remains possible if Bersatu candidates actively damage PN partners in the 8 Tier-4 seats (N.04/05/13/14/23/25/31/34). The PIR-09 axis adds a THIRD disciplinary/credibility event: MCA Youth sec-gen Saw Yee Fung told to 'stay out of NS polls' (by MCA sec-gen Chong Sin Woon) over her BN-PN criticism — internal BN-component disciplinary silencing — PLUS Albert Tei (PN camp, Jelebu) 'barking dogs' abrasive rhetoric toward PH supporters at the Dewan Besar Kuala Klawang nomination centre. The PIR-07 axis confirms the BN-PN electoral understanding is operational and PAS-led (Hadi/Asyraf Wajdi/Zahid cluster), surfaces DAP anti-BN intra-unity tension (1MDB, ruler crisis), and notes Samsuri's post-election BN-continuation intent (contingent on NS result). The 182751 cycle (02:27 MYT 19 Jul) is a STABILITY/CONFIRMATION pass: 0 new articles, 10/10 sources, 0 errors — the escalated-watch assessment is unchanged from 170837. No entity exceeds the 30% numeric sentiment-shift threshold in the rev4→rev5 delta (largest tracked: ESC-014 +7.9%, ESC-011 +6.7%, Bersatu +5.7%); however, the PIR-06 qualitative escalation (one-sided suspended-VP advocacy → two-sided/three-sided principal-level exit signaling) is FLAGGED as a CRITICAL sentiment shift per director directive. No nomination-period trigger fired; trigger #2 (coalition restructuring) is CLOSER than rev4 — now reachable via EITHER path (PN-MT removal OR Bersatu exit); trigger #7 (independents in 5-cornered seats N.10/N.33) on WATCH. Net NS-relevant sentiment remains PH-favorable: the opposition is now fractured on all three sides simultaneously, and the Bersatu president's exit-hint is the strongest signal yet that the coalition restructuring is in motion — maximally benefiting the incumbent. CLASSIFICATION: TLP:AMBER.
